text: Thyroid hormone signaling coordinates cell fate of photoreceptors in the visual system, especially during development and growth. Although different taxonomic groups differ in their photoreceptor subtypes, in general across species, thyroid hormone action promotes a shift of spectral sensitivity of opsins toward longer wavelengths. Decreased serum levels of triiodothyronine (T3), the more biologically active thyroid hormone, can alter photoreceptor patterning.
